name: Chantada - A Cova Loop
description: This moderate 25.8-mile (41.5 km) road cycling route in Ribeira Sacra offers 2814 feet (858 metres) of elevation gain over 2 hours and 18 mi
region_intro: Road cycling around O Saviñao features a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes and river valleys, particularly within the Ribeira Sacra region. The area offers varied terrain, including routes that follow riverside paths and cross numerous bridges over the Miño River and its tributaries. Cyclists can expect a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents through the Galician countryside, with mountain landscapes, vineyards, and forests of chestnut and oak trees. This diverse topography provides a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around O Saviñao The most popular road cycling route is Chantada - A Cova Loop, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route features substantial elevation gain through the area. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is A Cova Riverside Beach – Plaza de Galicia, Chantada loop from Chantada, a difficult 43.2 miles (69.5 km) path. This route navigates along riverside areas and through hilly terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Puente de Mourulle – San Fiz Bridge loop from Chantada, a 43.7 miles (70.3 km) trail leading through challenging climbs and scenic river valleys, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around O Saviñao is defined by challenging climbs, scenic river valleys, and bridge crossings. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in O Saviñao are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore O Saviñao's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in O Saviñao?
A: O Saviñao offers a diverse selection of over 40 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. The region is known for its significant elevation changes and scenic river valleys, providing a rewarding experience for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in O Saviñao?
A: Road cycling in O Saviñao features a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes and river valleys. You'll encounter a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents, often following riverside paths and crossing numerous bridges through the Galician countryside.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in O Saviñao?
A: Yes, O Saviñao is well-suited for cyclists seeking a challenge. Nearly half of the routes, 21 out of 44, are classified as difficult. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158659" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Cova Riverside Beach – Plaza de Galicia, Chantada loop from Chantada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 43.2-mile (69.5 km) path navigating hilly terrain and riverside areas.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Absolutely. O Saviñao offers 23 moderate road cycling routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942527477/chantada-a-cova-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chantada - A Cova Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.8-mile (41.5 km) trail with substantial elevation gain, typically completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the region's natural beauty. You might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5016863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6496544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra River Valley&lt;/a&gt;, or the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5795615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño River&lt;/a&gt;. These natural monuments provide excellent spots for a break or photo opportunity.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to explore near the cycling paths?
A: Yes, the area around O Saviñao is rich in history and culture. Cyclists can discover landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/751631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portomarín Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/137061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St John of Portomarín&lt;/a&gt;, or traditional structures like an &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4573085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo&lt;/a&gt;. These attractions offer a glimpse into the region's heritage.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in O Saviñao?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in O Saviñao are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Galicia, Chantada – Chantada Town Center loop from Chantada&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.9-mile (72.3 km) trail, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Mourulle – San Fiz Bridge loop from Chantada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in O Saviñao?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in O Saviñao, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and the scenic beauty of the river valleys and bridge crossings.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in O Saviñao?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant, offering comfortable conditions for tackling the varied terrain.

Q: Are there any less crowded or 'hidden gem' routes for road cycling in O Saviñao?
A: While popular routes like the Chantada loops are well-trafficked, exploring some of the longer, more challenging options can lead to quieter experiences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago Way Marker – Alto do Hospital loop from O Lugar do Vimieiro&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers a substantial 53.6-mile (86.3 km) journey with significant elevation, potentially providing a more secluded ride through the landscape.

Located between the River Miño Canyon and the Sierra Dorsal Galega, with Mount Faro as its summit, its beautiful old quarter and numerous examples of Romanesque architecture are striking. Considered the "heart of Galicia", it offers a variety of landscapes, mixing mountain and riverside landscapes. Among its attractions, a rich and impressive Historical-Artistic heritage stands out, in which the Romanesque style predominates.

Incredible place nestled in Lugo's Ribeira Sacra of the Miño River, without a doubt a must for anyone who likes nature. His journey to the town of Belesar is a delight and the climb to Escairon to see O Cabo do Mundo is a challenge for any athlete. One of the most recommended in the area.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Chantada - The Cova Vuelta route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, Chantada, as a town, offers various parking options. A good starting point would be near the Plaza de Galicia, Chantada, which is a central location and a highlight on the route. From there, you can easily begin your cycling adventure.

faqs.question: Is the Chantada - The Cova Vuelta route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, which typically means dogs are not suitable companions for the ride itself, especially on public roads. If you plan to visit the area with a dog, it's best to explore walking trails or parks where dogs are explicitly permitted, rather than bringing them on this specific cycling route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Galicia, including Chantada and Ribeira Sacra, is known for its 'green Spain' landscape. Generally, the best time for road cycling is during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months when the weather is milder and more stable. Summers can be warm, and winters may bring cooler temperatures and more rain.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route in Ribeira Sacra?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle the Chantada - The Cova Vuelta route. It is a public road cycling route within the Ribeira Sacra region, which is generally open for recreational activities without specific permits for cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Chantada - The Cova Vuelta route is classified as moderate. This means it requires a good level of fitness and is generally manageable for experienced cyclists. Beginners might find the 857 meters of elevation gain over 41.5 kilometers challenging, so it's recommended for those with some road cycling experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: As a racebike route, the terrain primarily consists of well-paved road surfaces. However, some segments in the broader region of Galicia can occasionally feature unpaved or more challenging sections. Expect rolling hills and ascents, given the significant elevation gain, offering a varied and engaging ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can experience several notable points of interest. These include the scenic Monforte to Belesar Road, the challenging Esteirón Ascent, the central Plaza de Galicia, Chantada, and the relaxing A Cova Riverside Beach.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camiño de Inverno, Ruta dos Viñedos de Belesar, and Paseo fluvial do Río Asma. You may also encounter parts of the Camino de Santiago de Invierno and Paseo do Sangoñedo.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While specific cafes directly on the route are not detailed, the route passes through or near Chantada, where you will find various cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. The Plaza de Galicia, Chantada is a good central point for finding amenities.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a comfortable ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given it's a moderate road cycling route, you should bring your racebike in good condition, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water bottles, and some snacks. A basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers) is also advisable. Don't forget your phone for navigation and emergencies, and consider a small first-aid kit.

faqs.question: Is this route part of the famous 'La Vuelta a España' race?
faqs.answer: No, the "Chantada - The Cova Vuelta" route is a local road cycling route with a moderate rating. It is distinct from the legendary and extremely difficult climbs often featured in the professional cycling race "La Vuelta a España," which are known for their steep gradients and significant elevation changes.
